data.title: Nippon Steel alleges White House had undue influence over US Steel review - Reuters
data.description:
Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) alleged that the Biden administration had "impermissible undue influence" over a national security review of its more than $14 billion planned acquisition of US Steel (NYSE:X).

The Japanese steelmaker made the allegations to the Committee on Foreign Investment in the U.S. on Tuesday, according to a Reuters report on Friday, which cited a letter that was also signed by US Steel (X).

CFIUS has a Monday deadline to decide if it will approve the deal, extend its review, or recommend that President Joe Biden blocks it. A possible government shutdown may pause the clock, CFIUS lawyers told Reuters.

"We are gravely concerned that the December 14 Letter reflects impermissible influence in the CFIUS process from the White House, at the behest of third parties who oppose the transaction and seek to weaken the Parties, namely, Cleveland-Cliffs..., led by CEO Lourenco Goncalves, and Mr. McCall," Nippon and US Steel said in the letter.

White House spokesperson Saloni Sharma said the allegation "is not true," according to Reuters. "The President has been clear that he will continue to see what the CFIUS process yields."

If Biden blocks the transaction, the parties plan to file an appeal immediately challenging the decision "as a violation of due process and the authority of the President," Nippon Steel wrote.

Shares of US Steel (X) fell 5% on Friday ahead of the CFIUS Monday deadline and after the steelmaker lowered guidance for its fourth quarter EBITDA, saying that demand and pricing in Europe remains weak.

data.title: Boston Beer updates 2024 guidance to reflect supplier contract amendment
data.description:
• The Boston Beer Company (NYSE:SAM), announced on Friday, an amendment and restatement in its entirety of an existing production agreement with a third-party supplier, Rauch North America.
  • This amendment adjusts the existing production agreement to better match the company’s future capacity requirements and results in increased production flexibility in exchange for a $26 million cash payment to Rauch.
  • As a result of the payment, the company expects to record a pre-tax contract settlement expense of $26 million or $1.70 after tax per diluted share impact in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • For the full year 2024, the company continues to estimate shortfall fees will negatively impact gross margin by 65 to 75 basis points and the non-cash expense of third-party production pre- payments will negatively impact gross margins by 95 to 105 basis points.
  • The company does not expect this agreement to materially impact its previously provided estimate of $14 million in 2025 shortfall fees disclosed in its third quarter 10-Q filed on October 24, 2024.
Full Year 2024 Current Guidance Prior Guidance
Depletions and Shipments Percentage Decrease Down low single digits Down low single digits
Price Increases 2% 2%
Gross Margin 44% to 45% 44% to 45%

data.title: FSK prices public offering of $100M 6.125% notes due 2030
data.description:
• FS KKR Capital (NYSE:FSK) said on Friday that it has priced an underwritten public offering of an additional $100 million in aggregate principal amount of its 6.125% notes due 2030.
  • The Notes will mature on January 15, 2030 and may be redeemed in whole or in part at FSK's option at any time at par plus a "make-whole" premium, provided that the Notes may be redeemed at par one month prior to their maturity.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 27, 2024, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • FSK intends to use the net proceeds of this offering for general corporate purposes, including potentially repaying outstanding indebtedness under credit facilities and certain notes.
  • FS KKR Capital (FSK) shares were down 0.85% in post-market trading.

data.title: Fennec's Pedmarqsi gets nod from U.K. regulators
data.description:
Fennec Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:FENC) said its marketing partner, Norgine Pharmaceuticals, has received positive final draft guidance from U.K. regulators for its drug Pedmarqsi.

The company said the U.K.'s National Institute for Health and Care Excellence is recommending Pedmarqsi for the prevention of cisplatin-induced hearing loss in patients aged one month to 17 years with localized, non-metastatic, solid tumors.

Earlier this year, Fennec (NASDAQ:FENC) entered into an exclusive licensing agreement with Norgine for the commercialization of Pedmarqsi in Europe, Australia, and New Zealand.

data.title: Lowe's Companies snapped eight consecutive sessions of declines
data.description:
Lowe's Companies (NYSE:LOW) shares snapped eight straight sessions of losses, as the stock closed 1.3% higher at $247.72 on Friday.

The home improvement retailer lost nearly 9.5% in the preceding eight sessions. The stock has added 10.7% so far this year, compared to the near 25% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

LOW is down 6% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, LOW has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, B- in momentum, while it got a D growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 21 analysts gave LOW a Buy and above rating, while 14 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation. Three analysts recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Earlier in November, LOW unveiled its 2025 Total Home Strategy at an investor event on December 11 in New York City. While the macroeconomic environment is still uncertain, Lowe's is confident that recent investments position it favorably for share gains once the home improvement market inflects. The company also made a point to emphasize that its rural footprint will grow.

“Despite a recent revenue decline, Lowe’s has a history of consistent long-term growth, supported by geographic expansion and organic growth in products and services,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: Desktop Metal files lawsuit against Nano Dimension for breach of merger agreement
data.description:
• Desktop Metal (DM) on Friday said it has filed a lawsuit against Nano Dimension in the Delaware Court of Chancery.
  • Desktop Metal has alleged that Nano Dimension has breached its obligation to use reasonable best efforts to obtain regulatory approval as soon as practicable in connection with the merger agreement between the parties.
  • The Court has scheduled an expedited hearing on December 30, 2024, to hear Desktop Metal’s motion for an expedited trial to be held in January.
  • Desktop Metal seeks specific performance, requiring Nano Dimension to cooperate in seeking approval by the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS), including finalizing negotiations and executing the mitigation agreement proposed by CFIUS and to close the merger within five business days of receiving CFIUS’ approval.
  • Desktop Metal also seeks a declaration of the Court that Nano Dimension has failed to perform its obligations to use reasonable best efforts to obtain regulatory approval and remains obligated to use its reasonable best efforts to take all actions necessary to consummate the merger as soon as reasonably possible.
  • All other regulatory conditions have been satisfied under the Merger Agreement, and the remaining regulatory condition to closing the merger is receipt of CFIUS approval.
  • Desktop Metal is confident that a closing of the merger may be completed expeditiously.
  • Eariler in July, Nano Dimension (NASDAQ:NNDM) had announced a deal to acquire Desktop Metal (DM) in an all-cash transaction for $5.50 a share, subject to possible downward adjustments to $4.07 per share.
  • NNDM closed +0.40% at $2.5.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Volkswagen reaches agreement with IG Metall after 70 hours of negotiations
data.description:
After 70 hours of marathon negotiations, Volkswagen (VWAGY, VWAPY) and the German union representing one-third of the carmaker’s workforce have come to an agreement that will avert a work stoppage.

The deal between Germany’s largest employer and the powerful IG Metall union would save Volkswagen (VWAGY, VWAPY) €15B annually by cutting manufacturing capacity by more than 700K at plants across the country. While the company agreed to keep most plants running, the Dresden factory will be shuttered at the end of 2025 and the Osnabrueck facility will remain open only until mid-2027. The two sides also agreed to move production of the VW Golf from Wolfsburg to Mexico.

The agreement would also eliminate 35K jobs through early retirement and buyouts by 2030, cancel bonus payments and suspend a 5% wage increase agreed to in November, while leaving monthly wage levels intact.

The head negotiator for IG Metall said the deal delivered “painful concessions” for workers, but the two sides “succeeded in finding a solution for employees that secures jobs, safeguards products in the plants and at the same time enables important future investments.”

Added Volkswagen CEO Oliver Blume, “With the package of measures that has been agreed, the company has set a decisive course for its future in terms of costs, capacities and structures.”

Earlier this month, the company warned that with a razor-thin margin of 2.1%, efforts to thwart cost-cutting measures and secure higher wages for workers would torpedo the company’s efforts to remain competitive.

“If we remain at this level, we will be unable to finance our future,” said Volkswagen (VWAGY, VWAPY) personnel chief Arne Meiswinkle, adding that the company is unable to meet IG Metall’s demands to reinstate the terminated collective agreements. For their part, the union threatened a full-scale strike if demands were not met.

Volkswagen (VWAGY, VWAPY) shares ended the day more than 3% higher and unchanged for the week.

data.title: Jury finds Qualcomm did not breach Nuvia's license agreement with Arm
data.description:
• A federal jury found that Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) did not breach Nuvia's license agreement with Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM).
  • The jury added that Qualcomm's custom CPUs, which are used in Microsoft (MSFT) PCs, are licensed legally as part of its existing deal with Arm.
  • Qualcomm shares rose 3.5% in extended trading on Friday, while Arm tumbled 2.7%.
  • The spat stems from whether Qualcomm-owned Nuvia breached a licensing agreement with Arm. While Nuvia and Qualcomm are licensors of Arm's technology, Qualcomm believed its acquisition of Nuvia would allow it to pay lower royalties than it had previously, something Arm objected to.
  • Arm sued Qualcomm in 2022 over the matter and canceled its chip license earlier this year as a result of the matter.
  • San Diego-based Qualcomm purchased Nuvia in March 2021 for $1.4B and said at the time it expected to integrate Nuvia's products "across a wide portfolio of products," including smartphones, laptops, advanced driver assistance systems and infrastructure networking solutions.

This is breaking news...

data.title: Rhythm gets FDA approval for Imcivree for toddlers
data.description:
Rhythm Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:RYTM) has received expanded approval for its drug Imcivree for use in children as young as two years old with certain types of genetic obesity.

The product is now approved for the reduction of excess body weight and maintenance of weight reduction in children with syndromic or monogenic obesity due to Bardet-Biedl syndrome, or BBS, or genetically confirmed pro-opiomelanocortin , or POMC, including proprotein convertase subtilisin/kexin type 1, or PCSK1, deficiency or leptin receptor, or LEPR, deficiency, according to a statement.

Imcivree, also known as setmelanotide, initially received approval from the FDA in November 2020 for patients six years old and older with POMC, PCSK1 or LEPR deficiencies. It was approved in June 2022 for patients with BBS.

data.title: Phillips 66 snaps eight straight days of losses
data.description:
Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) shares snapped eight straight sessions of losses, as the stock closed 0.02% higher, at $110.37 on Friday.

The Texas-headquartered energy company lost more than 12% in the last eight trading sessions. The stock has lost more than 16% so far this year, compared to an over 24% gain in the broader S&P 500 Index.

PSX is down more than 16% over the past one month.

Phillips 66 (PSX) said earlier this week that it has agreed to sell its 25% stake in the Gulf Coast Express pipeline in Texas to an affiliate of ArcLight Capital Partners for $865M in cash.

The deal means it has exceeded its $3B asset divestiture target, after previously selling fuel terminals, pipelines and a stake in a Switzerland retail joint venture to raise $2.7B.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating has rated the stock a HOLD, with a score of 2.87 out of 5. SA authors and the sell-side analysts, on the other hand, consider the stock a BUY.

Seeking Alpha analyst Michael Fitzsimmons points out that Phillips 66’s strategic shift to diversify away from refining to midstream and chemicals has had mixed results, with chemical expansions not yielding expected profits.

“Despite challenges, Phillips 66 has maintained strong dividend growth, but future profitability is threatened by potential tariffs and the evolving energy landscape,” Fitzsimmons said.
